frame_analyses.description: Instructional frame showing proper needle grasp technique
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational overlay showing needle should be grasped at 2/3 from tip, perpendicular to needle driver

frame_analyses.description: Needle entering foam tissue at 90 degrees with assistance from other instrument
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle angle and tissue handling technique demonstrated

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through tissue, both instruments visible for tissue manipulation
Suture being pulled through tissue creating proper bite
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Adequate suture length being pulled through

frame_analyses.description: Instructional frame showing C-loop formation and wrapping technique
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tutorial demonstrates proper surgeon's knot with double wrap around instrument

frame_analyses.description: Grasping short end to complete first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper technique for securing double throw (surgeon's knot)

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being performed with single wrap
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Correct alternating technique - single wrap for second throw

frame_analyses.description: C-loop formation for second throw with single wrap instruction
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good demonstration of proper throw alternation

frame_analyses.description: Final knot tightening to complete suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure with good tissue approximation

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double wrap demonstrated
Single throw with proper hand alternation

drain_assessment.assessment_note: This is an instructional tutorial video, not a performance assessment

technique_summary: This is a high-quality instructional tutorial demonstrating excellent intracorporeal suturing technique with proper needle handling, tissue manipulation, and knot construction. The video shows ideal form for educational purposes.
improvement_suggestions: This is a tutorial video - no performance improvements needed
strengths: Excellent demonstration of surgeon's knot technique
Clear visualization of proper hand alternation
